opportunity.title: Show HN: An open-source safety net for home hemodialysis
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The agent-based architecture is smart—real-time anomaly detection in a closed-loop system is surprisingly hard to get right; you'll quickly encounter edge cases where simple thresholds fail, necessitating more sophisticated behavioral modeling. Consider a hierarchical approach, where lower-level agents handle immediate responses and a higher-level agent manages long-term trends and system-wide safety protocols. The "self-improving loop" will be critical; data drift in physiological signals necessitates continuous recalibration of the agent's understanding of "normal."
actions.reasoning: My work in building AI agents for complex task orchestration and safety nets for automated systems directly parallels the challenges presented by this home hemodialysis safety net project.
